and devoted to ridding Boston of whatever they
deem to be “evil.” They sometimes support
themselves with the money they pilfer from
“evildoers,” which provides even more motiva-
tion for them to target organized crime and other
profitably corrupt elements. These days, the
Promise almost considers hunting mages to be
less godly than rooting out criminals and cults.
They know the Awakened use Sleepers to do
their dirty work and want to “starve wizards
out,” and the money is better to boot. But now
that the Combat Zone, Boston’s red-light district,
is less dangerous than it used to be, the group is
bored, agitated and looking for a new crusade,
and may try to confront other Awakened directly.
The SMGThe SMGThe SMGThe SMGThe SMG: MIT’s Special Media Group is a Free
Council cabal devoted to computer science and
mathematics. It firmly believes that the universe
can be reduced to programming code — and
members run applications that seem to prove it.
The cabal is constantly wired with the cutting
edge in wearable computers. They’ve used these
devices and their own talents to exploit casinos
in Atlantic City and Las Vegas, but not so much
as to draw undue attention — yet.
The Red Word:The Red Word:The Red Word:The Red Word:The Red Word: Local legend tells of this
terrifying cabal: “The man was missing most of
his right arm. There were ragged, tooth-marked
flaps of flesh left. Similar gnawed wounds had
taken an eye and ear. His mind was gone, but
he managed to find his way to Davy Jones, a
man who he’d traded information and Mana
with a week before. The wounded mage
babbled about the Red Word, about cannibals
and languages that should never be spoken.
‘They have taken a whole sentence from me,’
said the mad mage, ‘and I am damned for
having known it.’ Then he turned a killing spell
on himself.” Since then, rumors of the Red Word
— cannibal occultists — stoke the fears of mages,
even though stories conflict and none of the
Awakened can claim to have ever seen
one of them.
The White Putnams
One of the two cabals with the oldest lineage in Boston,
the White Putnams have spent the past three centuries
transforming themselves from an obscure farming commune
to a fellowship riddled with Massachusetts gentry.
In 1905, two Awakened sisters who claimed to be descen-
dants of Samuel Parris moved to Boston and founded the
White Putnams. The cabal’s name was intended to reflect
their ancestry and to symbolize that they repudiated the sins
of the past. In a more practical vein, the sisters’ humility
made their allegiance to the Silver Ladder no threat to the
then-ascendant Guardians of the Veil. They devoted them-
selves to teaching, charity and diplomacy and kept the
Putnam name for their sanctum.
The cabal attempted to claim Salem in 1906, in a move to
both accept responsibility for its ancestors’ role in the city’s
past and to reclaim its heritage. The Ebon Noose opposed the
Putnams. The conflict resulted in the Secret Concord and
the Putnams’ retreat to Boston, although the curse they
believed had haunted them was now seemingly put to rest by
their signing of the Concord.
The modern White Putnam cabal doesn’t adhere to the
Puritan stereotype. Under Provost Chain Parris’ leadership,
it pursues a repentant policy of tolerance. Its seven mages do
not unanimously support the Provost’s position, however.
Three believe that the cabal has become too tolerant and
they are tired of cooperating with heathens and blasphemers.
The rest follow Parris’ lead. These three have thrown their
lot in with the Mysterium instead of the Silver Ladder of
cabal tradition.
The White Putnams shadow the kinds of parties and
meetings the rich descendants of Mayflower colonists hold.
When the elite need a streak of luck or an ironclad secret
revealed, they glance at the White Putnams’ brass ring, slink
to their side and bargain for services. Originally, the cabal
supported rising middle-class Sleepers, but as those ordinary
folks’ station among mages declines, cabal members comfort
themselves with the wealth and ritual of Sleeper high soci-
ety. Nevertheless, the cabal refuses jobs that don’t meet its
ethical standards and charges dearly for the ones it does
accept. The resulting fees and favors go to Christian charities
and to a constantly shifting array of projects.
The White Putnams have been known to hire visiting or
struggling mages for a number of tasks, including surveil-
lance, tracking smuggled artifacts and “correcting” the
behavior of other visiting mages.
Chain Parris
Quote: This is a lovely summer getaway, Senator. Looking out
at the water, we can almost forget about that boating accident.
What? Certainly — a charitable contribution does reflect genuine
penitence. Actually, I have a suggestion along those lines.
Background: Jonathan “Chain” Parris cuts a handsome
figure through upscale Massachusetts, a laughing man who’s
always just to the left of the spotlight at politically charged
weddings, regattas and similar events. There’s no need to
invite him to events; the proper people just know that he
should be there. He stands out among the idle rich because
of his simple, healthy lifestyle.
